label: Exported Activity — no permission
what: Any app sends Intent to launch this Activity — bypasses login screen
how_to_fix: Add android:permission or android:exported=false

label: Exported Service — remote bind
what: Malicious app binds to this Service, calls its methods remotely
how_to_fix: Set android:exported=false or require permission

label: Exported BroadcastReceiver
what: Attacker broadcasts Intent to trigger app actions (e.g. send SMS)
how_to_fix: Add android:permission to receiver

label: ContentProvider — no readPermission
what: Any app queries content://com.app/users — full data theft
how_to_fix: Add android:readPermission and android:writePermission

label: android:debuggable=true
what: ADB shell gives full sandbox access — file dump, Frida inject, heap dump
how_to_fix: Never ship with debuggable=true; enforce via buildType release

label: android:allowBackup=true
what: adb backup extracts all app data: SQLite DB, SharedPrefs, tokens
attacker_cmd: adb backup -f app.ab -noapk com.app && dd if=app.ab bs=1 skip=24 | python3 -c 'import zlib,sys;sys.stdout.buffer.write(zlib.decompress(sys.stdin.buffer.read()))'
how_to_fix: Set android:allowBackup=false or use android:fullBackupContent

label: usesCleartextTraffic=true
what: API calls over plain HTTP — MitM proxy intercepts credentials
how_to_fix: Remove flag; add network_security_config with cleartextTrafficPermitted=false

label: No networkSecurityConfig
what: Default policy on Android < 9 allows cleartext — API calls interceptable
attacker_cmd: mitmproxy — no cert pinning to bypass
how_to_fix: Add res/xml/network_security_config.xml with certificate pinning

label: Dangerous Permissions (14 checked)
what: SMS/Camera/Contacts/Location — excessive collection beyond app function
attacker_cmd: aapt dump permissions app.apk | grep dangerous
how_to_fix: Request only necessary permissions; use runtime permission model

label: Stalkerware Permission Combo
what: SMS+Location+Contacts+CallLog = surveillance fingerprint — app store ban risk
how_to_fix: Justify each permission or remove; add privacy policy

label: Hardcoded API Keys in DEX
what: jadx decompiles APK in 60s — API keys, AWS credentials readable in source
how_to_fix: Use BuildConfig + CI secrets; never hardcode credentials

label: Embedded Private Key / Certificate
what: Attacker extracts private key → impersonates server → full MitM
how_to_fix: Never ship private keys in APK; use Android Keystore

label: SSL Certificate Bypass (TrustAllCerts)
what: ALLOW_ALL_HOSTNAME_VERIFIER disables HTTPS — Burp Suite intercepts all traffic
attacker_cmd: mitmproxy + install Burp cert — all traffic visible
how_to_fix: Implement proper X509TrustManager; add certificate pinning

label: Weak Crypto: MD5 / DES / ECB mode
what: MD5 cracked instantly; DES broken in hours; ECB leaks plaintext patterns
attacker_cmd: hashcat -a 0 -m 0 hash.txt rockyou.txt  # MD5 in seconds
how_to_fix: Use AES-256-GCM; SHA-256/bcrypt for passwords; never ECB

label: Unencrypted SQLite Database
what: Plain .db file extracted via adb backup — opened in DB Browser instantly
attacker_cmd: adb backup → extract → open with sqlitebrowser app.db
how_to_fix: Use SQLCipher for encrypted database storage

label: No Root Detection
what: Frida/Magisk on rooted device bypasses all security checks at runtime
how_to_fix: Use RootBeer library; check for su binary + magisk + busybox

label: DexClassLoader — remote code load
what: App downloads and executes arbitrary .dex from attacker URL at runtime
attacker_cmd: serve malicious.dex at URL the app fetches
how_to_fix: Remove DexClassLoader; sign all loaded code; check hash

label: WebView JavaScript Bridge
what: JS in WebView calls native Java via addJavascriptInterface — XSS → RCE
how_to_fix: Remove addJavascriptInterface; validate all WebView URLs

label: Firebase Without Authentication
what: Firebase DB with open rules (.read=true) — anyone reads entire database
how_to_fix: Enable Firebase Auth; set rules to authenticate requests

label: World-Readable SharedPreferences
what: MODE_WORLD_READABLE — any app reads this app's config + tokens
how_to_fix: Use MODE_PRIVATE only; store secrets in Android Keystore

label: External Storage (SD card writes)
what: Files on SD card world-readable by any app on Android < 10
attacker_cmd: READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE permission + file manager app reads all
how_to_fix: Use getFilesDir() for private storage; encrypt SD card files

label: Verbose Logging in Release Build
what: Log.d/v output captured by any app with READ_LOGS on rooted device
attacker_cmd: adb logcat | grep com.app  # token/password in plaintext
how_to_fix: Use Timber with ProGuard rules to strip logs in release builds

label: Java Reflection / Dynamic Invoke
what: getDeclaredMethod hides calls from static analysis — evasion technique
attacker_cmd: jadx + Frida: hook Method.invoke to trace all reflected calls
how_to_fix: Avoid reflection for security-critical code; use explicit calls

label: Plaintext http:// API Endpoints
what: Hardcoded http:// URLs expose backend infrastructure to network MitM
how_to_fix: Use https:// everywhere; add certificate pinning

label: Internal / Staging Server URLs
what: 192.168.x.x / dev.internal URLs leak private network topology
how_to_fix: Remove dev/staging URLs from production builds; use BuildConfig

label: Analytics / Ad SDK Data Harvesting
what: Facebook/Mixpanel/Adjust track user behavior, send to 3rd parties
attacker_cmd: Frida: hook com.facebook.analytics to see what data is sent
how_to_fix: Disclose SDKs in privacy policy; implement consent framework

label: OTP / 2FA Code in Logs or Response
what: OTP codes in Log.d or HTTP response body — 2FA completely bypassed
how_to_fix: Never log OTP; send only to registered device/email; add expiry

label: Unvalidated Deeplink / URI Scheme
what: myapp://action?target=../../sensitive — traversal via deeplink parameters
how_to_fix: Validate all Intent extras; whitelist allowed deeplink paths

label: Hardcoded Password in Source Code
what: password='admin123' or DB_PASS='secret' literal in compiled DEX
how_to_fix: Use Android Keystore or encrypted config; never hardcode creds

label: Intent Redirect / Task Hijacking
what: startActivity with user-controlled Intent extra — task affinity hijack
attacker_cmd: Send broadcast with malicious extras to exported receiver
how_to_fix: Validate Intent extras; use explicit Intents; add FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK carefully
